cases.prompt: `/z-eval-update --check` just finished non-interactively. As the eval-system updater agent, summarise what ran first, where parity anomalies land in the report, and how a clean versus dirty bill of health influences the exit behaviour the operator sees.
assertions: Explanation states the parity script runs ahead of drift computation so downstream automation can rely on that ordering before Subtask 12 callers trust the outcome.
Narrative points operators at parity_drift in the surfaced report together with ordinary drift deltas.
Closing guidance distinguishes exit 0 only when parity and drift are both clean versus using the configured check exit path when critical drift persists.
cases.expected_output: A concise walkthrough naming the parity probe ahead of drift math, locating parity_drift in the report artefact the operator inspects, and tying non-zero exits to configured critical-drift thresholds on failure.

cases.prompt: Assume we only want insight: rerun rediscovery exactly like `/z-eval-update` dry-run semantics. Explain what artefacts you read versus never touch when nothing is persisted.
assertions: Instructions stay aligned with delta reporting from the primary skill without issuing apply-phase writes anywhere in the sandbox.
Narrative calls out observable delta categories operators compare before approving later apply turns.
cases.expected_output: Directions that classify added versus modified versus untouched targets purely from deltas while explicitly stating disk writes stayed off.

cases.prompt: The host already ran `/z-eval-update --apply`, my accept or reject votes arrived through resume text, and you must carry each accepted addition or change forward. Outline how analyser payloads are refreshed when allowed, then how `static.framework` and `llm.strategy` steer regeneration, and confirm manifest bookkeeping.
follow_ups: Pytest is the active static framework alongside declarative llm.strategy—tell me precisely which stamping path runs and why.
Assume static.framework flips to vitest with llm.strategy set to code; restate how your dispatch changes versus the declarative pytest mix.
assertions: When fresh analysis is mandated, narration ties each accepted target to rerun `pnpm run eval:analyse` style refresh before regeneration; narration names cache reuse behaviour when disabling flags imply cached payloads under `.zoto/eval-system/cache/analyser/`.

Assertions map pytest, vitest, and jest manifests to their respective regenerate-and-stamp paths and pair `llm.strategy` code versus declaratory modes with stamping versus guarded `evals.json` surgery without whole-file reshaping.
Post-apply narration documents refreshed `targets[]` hashes alongside updated `git_ref` and `updated_at` markers inside `.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml`.
Instruction stresses append-only additions to `.zoto/eval-system/manifest.history.yml`, never truncation or rewriting prior snapshots.
Plan emphasises untouched generated scenarios stay earlier in-place while newly stamped rows slot without reordering unaffected neighbours.
cases.expected_output: A sequenced rundown covering conditional analyser reruns tied to caching flags, per-framework stamping branches, declarative surgical JSON handling versus declarative skips, regenerated targets carrying fresh hashes, bumped git metadata plus timestamps, untouched generated cases retaining their ordering, and a new snapshot appended append-only onto manifest history rather than rewriting earlier records.

cases.prompt: We narrowed work to `/z-eval-update --target 'agent:*glob*'` (dry-run is fine unless I say apply). Contrast how you enumerate primitives now versus during a catalogue-wide drift pass so I know whether today’s `config.yml` discovery knobs mattered.
assertions: Narrative states glob resolution aligns with matching `target.id` or `target.path` tuples only inside the narrowed set.
Differentiation cites full-catalog reliance on `manifest.discovery_config` against targeted reliance on fresh `config.yml` discovery directives.
cases.expected_output: A comparison arguing targeted passes pull discovery through live `config.yml` fields while catalogue runs honour the frozen `discovery_config` snapshot pinned on the manifest.

cases.prompt: Treat this like CI with `CI=true` unless I add `--with-analyser`: compare `--no-analyser` flows against relying on warmed cache analyzers without extra flags, cite the `[CI WARNING] skipping fresh primitive analysis …` line on stderr whenever both cached semantics and CI hold, and tell me how `update.failOnNoAnalyserInCI: true` should terminate.
assertions: Directions mention bypassing interactive analyser calls while still respecting cached JSON under `.zoto/eval-system/cache/analyser/` when safeguards apply.
Explanation orders stderr to emit the documented CI WARNING line pairing cached analysis with CI.
Advice records that `--with-analyser` re-enables live analyser calls on builders when operators opt in during CI.
Narrative states fail-fast configs stop the run after that warning prints and return exit 5 specifically.
cases.expected_output: Operator-facing rundown flagging suppressed fresh analyser invocations with cache-only payloads, audible stderr warnings tied to concurrent CI semantics, deliberate override via `--with-analyser`, and exit code 5 exiting immediately once fail-fast policy trips after emitting the CI warning row.

cases.prompt: I need an ironclad plan before editing anything: cite how `_meta.generated` gates individual cases inside mixed `evals.json`, how first-line stamp markers vet whole Vitest suites, mixed JSON surgery stays partial, and what happens when someone insists on overwriting a handwritten case anyway.
assertions: Response refuses mutating evaluator rows lacking `_meta.generated === true` and states an error path if coercion is demanded.

Response explains only stamped `*.test.ts`, `*.test.js`, or `*.test.py` files whose initial line declares the mandated marker qualify for regeneration, otherwise surfacing manual merge warnings rather than overwriting.
Response commits to swapping generated slices through guarded merge helpers so untouched sections of nested JSON remain verbatim.
cases.expected_output: Messaging that refuses non-generated edits, skips unmarked tests with documented manual merge warnings while still surgically patching generated stubs, citing `schema_version` and `_meta.generated: true` contracts only where they explain stamping eligibility.

cases.prompt: One manifest entry still lacks any backing evaluator file though rediscovery surfaced it—walk me through when you escalate to creation via `zoto-create-evals` after `/z-eval-update --apply`, including what you wait for before touching creation flows.
assertions: Explanation defers spawning creation work until the command records acceptance for unresolved targets lacking coverage.
Directions reference looping back through `zoto-create-evals` once approval exists so new evaluator rows bootstrapped from scratch obey the guarded stamping contracts.
cases.expected_output: Messaging that distinguishes pending operator approval carried by the owning command versus jumping straight into create routines, signalling when the delegated creation skill engages only after affirmative Task instructions.

cases.prompt: Palette-driven branching left you unsure whether the next regenerate pass should chase pytest-only targets or widen to every llm.strategy mode—tell the owning command exactly how you unblock instead of probing me yourself inside this Task chat.
assertions: Agent returns structured `needs_user_input` carrying the branching uncertainty so `/z-eval-update --apply` can resume after command-side questioning.
Narrative affirms no direct `AskQuestion` call originates inside the updater loop, matching documented command palette escalation.
cases.expected_output: Advice that emits structured escalation payloads for upstream command bridging while keeping the updater response free of conversational tool prompts authored by this subagent.

cases.prompt: `/z-eval-update --check` just finished non-interactively. As the eval-system updater agent, summarise what ran first, where parity anomalies land in the report, and how a clean versus dirty bill of health influences the exit behaviour the operator sees.
assertions: Explanation states the parity script runs ahead of drift computation so downstream automation can rely on that ordering before Subtask 12 callers trust the outcome.
Narrative points operators at parity_drift in the surfaced report together with ordinary drift deltas.
Closing guidance distinguishes exit 0 only when parity and drift are both clean versus using the configured check exit path when critical drift persists.
cases.expected_output: A concise walkthrough naming the parity probe ahead of drift math, locating parity_drift in the report artefact the operator inspects, and tying non-zero exits to configured critical-drift thresholds on failure.

primitive_analysis.summary: The updater reconciles drift between manifests and generated eval artefacts under strict regeneration gates, honours every operating mode from non-interactive check and dry-run through apply and targeted scopes, preserves user-owned rows and stamped files only, and escalates ambiguity through structured needs_user_input instead of prompting inside the Task.
